channels.title: Channel Settings
channels.subtitle: View configured channels, account health, and runtime connectivity using Client-native UI.

dialog.description: Read-only channel status details. The backend currently exposes read endpoints here, so this desktop page intentionally avoids fake edit controls.
dialog.noAccounts: No accounts reported for this channel.

models.subtitle: Manage provider endpoints, credentials, and the model catalog exposed to desktop users.

dialog.description: Configure an API endpoint and the model names exposed through it. UI remains Client-native while functionality follows the Web product.
dialog.apiKeyKeepCurrent: API Key (leave blank to keep current)

sections.basicInfoDescription: Help 章鱼哥AI personalize responses to you.
sections.locationDescription: Enables location-aware responses like weather, local time, and regional context.
sections.notesDescription: Free-form context that helps 章鱼哥AI understand your preferences.

rows.name: Name
rows.nameDescription: How 章鱼哥AI should address you.
rows.timezoneDescription: Used for relative dates like 'tomorrow' or 'next week'.
rows.languageDescription: Preferred language for 章鱼哥AI's responses.
rows.cityDescription: Your city for local information and context.
rows.countryDescription: Your country for regional formatting and context.

placeholders.name: Your name
placeholders.notes: Any additional information you'd like to share with the AI assistant...

permissions.aboutP1: Permissions control how much autonomy your agent has. In Explore mode, the agent can only read and research, which is ideal for understanding a problem before making changes. When you are ready, switch to Execute mode to let the agent implement the plan autonomously.
permissions.aboutP2: A good workflow is to start in Explore, review the proposed plan, and then execute with confidence.
permissions.defaultDescription: App-level patterns allowed in Explore mode. Commands not on this list are blocked.
permissions.workspaceDescription: Agent-scoped rules that extend the app defaults above.
permissions.workspaceEmptyDescription: Create a {{file}} file in this agent's workspace to add custom rules.

labels.title: Labels
labels.aboutP1: Labels help you organize sessions with colored tags. Use them to categorize conversations by project, topic, or priority so related sessions are easier to find later.
labels.aboutP2: Each label can optionally carry a value with a specific type, such as text, number, or date. This turns labels into structured metadata.
labels.aboutP3: Auto-apply rules assign labels automatically when a message matches a regex pattern, so common workflows can be tagged without manual work.
labels.hierarchyDescription: All labels configured for this agent's workspace. Labels can be nested to form groups.
labels.hierarchyEmptyDescription: Labels can be created by the agent or by editing {{file}} in this agent's workspace.
labels.autoRulesDescription: Regex patterns that automatically apply labels when matched in user messages. For example, pasting a Linear issue URL can automatically tag the session with the project name and issue ID.

rows.name: Name
rows.noSources: No sources configured for this agent.

welcome.descriptionNew: Agents with the UX they deserve. Connect anything. Organize your sessions. Everything you need to do the work of your life!
welcome.descriptionExisting: Update your API connection or change your setup.

apiSetup.title: Set up your Agent
apiSetup.description: Select how you'd like to power your AI agents. You can add more connections later.
apiSetup.segmentDescriptionAnthropic: Use Claude Agent SDK as the main agent. Configure it with your Claude subscription or API key.
apiSetup.segmentDescriptionPi: Use 章鱼哥AI Backend as the main agent. Configure it with your API key, OAuth subscription, or custom endpoint.
apiSetup.optionClaudeDescription: Use your Claude subscription for unlimited access.
apiSetup.optionAnthropicApiDescription: Pay-as-you-go via Anthropic, OpenRouter, or compatible APIs.
apiSetup.optionChatgptDescription: Use your ChatGPT subscription with 章鱼哥AI Backend.
apiSetup.optionCopilotDescription: Use your GitHub Copilot subscription with 章鱼哥AI Backend.
apiSetup.optionApiDescription: Use provider presets (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) via 章鱼哥AI Backend.

credentials.chatgptDescription: Use your ChatGPT subscription to power 章鱼哥AI.
credentials.chatgptBody: Click the button above to sign in with your OpenAI account. A browser window will open for authentication.
credentials.chatgptConnected: Connected! Your ChatGPT subscription is ready.

credentials.clickSignInGithub: Click the button above to sign in with your GitHub account.
credentials.copilotConnected: Connected! Your GitHub Copilot subscription is ready.
credentials.authCodeDescription: Copy the code from the browser page and paste it below.
credentials.claudeDescription: Use your Claude subscription to power multi-agent workflows.
credentials.apiDescriptionPi: Select a provider preset and enter the API key. For arbitrary Anthropic-compatible endpoints, use Anthropic API Key mode.
credentials.apiDescriptionAnthropic: Enter your API key. Optionally configure a custom endpoint for OpenRouter, Ollama, or compatible APIs.

localModel.title: Local Model
localModel.description: Connect to Ollama or any OpenAI-compatible local server.
localModel.endpointHint: Default Ollama port is 11434. Change it if you use a custom setup.
localModel.modelRequired: At least one model name is required.
localModel.modelHint: Use any model pulled via ollama pull. Use commas to provide multiple models.

gitBash.title: Git Bash Required
gitBash.description: 章鱼哥AI needs Git Bash to run shell commands on Windows. It was not found on your system.
gitBash.installDescription: The easiest way to get Git Bash. It's free and includes everything you need.
gitBash.alreadyInstalledDescription: If Git is installed in a non-standard location, you can specify the path to bash.exe.

sessions.noArchivedDescription: Sessions you archive will appear here. Archive sessions to keep your list tidy while preserving conversations.
sessions.noSessionsDescription: Sessions with your agent appear here. Start one to get going.

agents.introDescription: Use the middle panel to browse Local and Remote groups. The details view here follows the currently selected local agent.
agents.emptySelection: Select a local agent from the middle panel to inspect its environment details.

remote.title: Remote agents
remote.pendingConnected: Server transport is wired. Remote agent list API is the next integration step.
remote.pendingUnavailable: Remote agent list will appear here once the client is connected to a server-side agent API.
remote.descriptionUnavailable: Remote transport status is unavailable. Remote agent listing can be connected here once the desktop client has a server-side agent API.
remote.descriptionLocalMode: This client is currently running in local mode. Remote agents will appear here once remote transport and server-side agent APIs are enabled.
remote.descriptionConnected: Remote transport is connected. The remaining integration is the server-side remote agent list and detail API.
remote.descriptionFallback: Remote transport is not ready yet. Retry the connection after the remote server is available.

skills.emptyDescription: Skills are reusable instructions that teach your agent specialized behaviors.

sources.description: Sources connect your agent to external data — MCP servers, REST APIs, and local folders.
